title: Feature: Change-aware criteria cache (scout-side Tier 1 + Tier 2 consumer)
summary: Replace scout's dumb-TTL invalidation of the cached company-fit brief with the consumer half of the change-propagation cost cascade. The brain side is already built (GET /changes?since=<cursor> Tier 0 signal + the toolkit onChange seam, brainbot 5dbc604). Scout now: stores the brain's opaque cursor + a verified_at stamp beside the cached brief; on resolve, asks /changes whether anything moved (Tier 0) and, only if it did, re-runs the recall gather to compare the distill basis (Tier 1) before paying for LLM synthesis (Tier 2); and replaces the lying age>=TTL 'stale' badge with an honest Current/Unverified/Changed tri-state. Net: steady state is one cheap /changes call per resolve, zero LLM spend and zero brief-prose wobble until the brain changes in a way that touches the criteria. Source of truth: brainbot/docs/change-propagation.md + brainbot/docs/consumer-api.md (the /changes contract).
outOfScope: The brain side of the cascade — the /changes cursor endpoint and the L3 toolkit onChange wrapper are already built (brainbot 5dbc604); this feature is the scout consumer only
True push transport (SSE/webhooks) — deferred by design; the toolkit's onChange seam holds the upgrade. Scout polls /changes; do not build a bus
Changing verdict scoring, the distiller's prompts, or distillation quality — the basis/version semantics are reused as-is, not redesigned
Changing the re-scoring policy — verdicts still do not auto-re-score on a criteria change; a real basis change still bumps Version exactly as today (see internal/verdict/verdict.go:253)
taste.md offline-fallback behavior — unchanged; it remains the last resort when the brain is unreachable and no cache exists
technicalConsiderations: The /changes contract (brainbot/docs/consumer-api.md): GET /changes?since=<cursor> -> {cursor: str, changed: bool}. cursor is OPAQUE — compare for equality only, never parse. changed=true when since is absent or stale, false when it matches. The cursor is backed by count(*)+max(updated_at) over sources and is deliberately COARSER than /doc's version: a no-op re-sync advances it. So the cursor is a 're-check now' trigger, NOT proof scout's specific view changed — the recompute MUST be gated on the distill basis (Tier 1), never on the cursor alone.

The basis already exists: distill.Result.Basis / brain_profile_cache.content_hash = a hash over BOTH distiller prompts + the recalled chunk content (distill.basisOf). It depends only on the recall gather, NOT on classify/synthesize output — so it is computable right after the recall fan-out and before any LLM call. That is what makes Tier 1 (skip synthesis when the basis is unchanged) possible without a second recall.

Tier 1 needs a two-phase distiller: gather(recall) -> basis, then synthesize(gathered) -> brief, so the basis can be compared before paying for synthesis and without re-running recall. Today distill.Distiller.Distill() does gather+classify+synthesize in one shot; the BriefSource interface (internal/criteria/resolver.go:46) must be widened. Keep a whole-pipeline path for `scout distill` and Refresh().

Resolve() is called roughly once per scoring run (criteria are resolved per-run, not per-company), so a /changes call per Resolve is trivially cheap — no debounce/throttle needed. TTL stops being the Tier 0 trigger and survives only as the staleness ceiling for serving a cached brief when the brain is unreachable, and as an input to the 'unverified' display state.

The no-op brief-wobble bug this fixes: today TTL expiry re-runs synthesis even when nothing moved, and synthesis is not byte-stable, so the visible brief prose drifts on a no-op refresh. Skipping synthesis when changed=false OR the basis is unchanged is exactly what stops the wobble; the cached body is returned verbatim.

Store: brain_profile_cache currently has (source_url, body, content_hash, fetched_at). Add cursor + verified_at. PutBrainProfile (full write) stamps verified_at=now and the new cursor; add a TouchBrainProfile(sourceURL, cursor) that updates cursor+verified_at WITHOUT rewriting body/content_hash — the Tier 0/Tier 1 'confirmed unchanged' stamp. Next migration number is 0037 (highest existing is 0036).

UI lives in the Vite TS PWA under web/, built to internal/web/dist and go:embed'd. Per the worktree memory: branches base off (possibly stale) origin/master — rebuild dist and restore the node_modules symlink before building. The criteria panel is where the current 'stale' pill renders.
Brain client (internal/brainbot/client.go) follows a getJSON(ctx, path, query, out) pattern — add Changes() alongside Map()/Doc()/Recall() the same way. Enabled()/Health() already exist.
